def: s.f. Piling up (stones, bricks, &c.), building up (a wall); masonry-work; — filling up (a hole) by ramming (stones, &c.) into it; ramming, cramming
subs: s.f. Building with bricks (or stone) and mortar, brick-and-mortar work
v.n. To ram, cram, stuff (oneself), to eat much without drinking

def: adv. = ćunāṅ.
adj. & adv. Like that, such as that, such; so
subs: adv. In this and that manner; — s.f. Evasion, subterfuge; talk, palaver; bombast, grandiloquence; — flaws, defects
To practise evasion; to find fault (with), pick holes (in)
ćunāṅ-će, ćunāṅ-ki or -ke, adv. In such a manner that, so that, as, like as, accordingly, for example.

def: s.m. A gather, pucker, rumple, crease.
v.t. To cause to pick, or gather, or cull, &c.; to cause to be chosen or selected; to cause to be arranged, or laid, or put in order; — to dress, array, or deck (oneself) with
subs: v.t. To plait (cloth), to crimp; to kill by building (a person) into a wall, to enclose alive and build in, to immure ( = ćinwānā).

def: s.f. = ćunrī, q.v. s.f. A mode of dying cloth parti-coloured (knots being tied in different parts of it previous to dipping it in the dye to prevent those parts from receiving the colour; syn. ćuṭkī); cloth dyed parti-coloured; a parti-coloured sheet or sāṛī (worn by women).
s.m. A lime-burner; a worker in lime.
s.m. A lime-burner; a worker in lime.
s.f. Plaiting (cloth), crimping; plait, gather, ruffle; rumple, crease.
s.f. A small spoon used to take out the lime.
s.f. A small spoon used to take out the lime.

s.f. Gathering, folding; folding the sheet or mantle (sāṛī); — selection of the fittest men (from an army) for a desperate enterprise; incitement, encouragement (of troops in battle, for which purpose an officer is especially appointed in Indian armies); defiance, provocacation; — a practice which formerly prevailed at Benares (viz. on the eleventh day of the śukl-paksh of Jaiṭh the inhabitants were wont to swim across the river, and forming themselves into two parties, to fight with swords, clubs, &c.).
s.f. Plaiting, crimping; plait, gather ( = ćunat).
s.f. See s.v. ćūṅ. s.f. A slight or low noise or sound; squeaking, a squeak; creaking; the sound of breaking wind
subs: ćūṅ-ćūṅ, ćūṅ-ćahāṭ, s.f. Creaking; squeaking; chirruping, chirrup (of birds, grasshoppers, &c.); twittering, twitter; warbling, song, voice
s.m. Making a suppressed sound or noise; the sound of breaking wind
To make not the slightest noise, to remain perfectly quiet.

def: s.m. = ćuććaṛ, q.v. s.m. A large breast; a large udder.
s.f. = ćūćī, q.v.

s.m. Making a suppressed sound or noise; the sound of breaking wind
To make not the slightest noise, to remain perfectly quiet.

def: s.m. The worm Ascaris ( = ćunnā)
subs: v.n. To be afflicted with Ascarides; — (fig.) to be restless, to be on thorns.

def: s.f. The worm Ascaris; — itchiness, uneasiness (cf. ćulćul).
adj. & s.m. Very little, sparing, paltry; niggardly, miserly; — a niggard, a miser ( = ćanṭ).
s.f. = ćundarī, ćunrī, q.v.
s.f. = ćundarī, ćunrī, q.v.

ety: S. चक+अन्ध+कः, and ल+कः, cf. ćūṅdhā; ćakāćaundh, &c.
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Having weak eyes, dim-sighted, purblind; — indistinct, bad, scrawly (handwriting); — one whose eyes are too weak to bear daylight, a blinkard.
v.n. To be dazzled by excess of light = ćauṅdhiyānā), to see dimly (owing to weak eyes), to be purblind.

ety: S. चक+अन्ध+कः, and ल+कः, cf. ćūṅdhā; ćakāćaundh, &c.
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Having weak eyes, dim-sighted, purblind; — indistinct, bad, scrawly (handwriting); — one whose eyes are too weak to bear daylight, a blinkard.
v.n. To be dazzled by excess of light = ćauṅdhiyānā), to see dimly (owing to weak eyes), to be purblind.
s.f. A harlot, a bawd, a procuress.
s.f. = ćundī, q.v. s.f. A harlot, a bawd, a procuress.

s.f. = ćunrī, q.v. s.f. A mode of dying cloth parti-coloured (knots being tied in different parts of it previous to dipping it in the dye to prevent those parts from receiving the colour; syn. ćuṭkī); cloth dyed parti-coloured; a parti-coloured sheet or sāṛī (worn by women).

s.f. = ćunrī, q.v. s.f. A mode of dying cloth parti-coloured (knots being tied in different parts of it previous to dipping it in the dye to prevent those parts from receiving the colour; syn. ćuṭkī); cloth dyed parti-coloured; a parti-coloured sheet or sāṛī (worn by women).

s.f. A cess levied from grain-sellers, &c. (being as much grain as a man can grasp in his hand); the fees of a weigher of grain; town-duties; illegal abstractions of handfuls of market produce (frequently, however, given as a sort of rent for the use of market conveniences, or to zamīn-dārs, baniyās, &c. for the establishment of new markets)
subs: s.f. A market or fair held on condition of giving a small portion of each saleable article to the zamīndār.

def: s.f. = ćuṅgī, q.v. s.f. A cess levied from grain-sellers, &c. subs: s.f. A market or fair held on condition of giving a small portion of each saleable article to the zamīndār.

def: v.t. = ćugnā, q.v. v.t. To pick up food (with the beak), to pick. peck; to feed
subs: v.t. To pick up; — to gather, cull, select, choose ( = ćunnā); to fold, to plait.

part. adj. corr. of ćinīda, q.v. part. adj. Gathered, picked, culled, selected, chosen; select, choice.

subs: s.f. A duck (as disting. fr. a goose).

def: s.m. Sorrel, &c. ( = ćūk, q.v.). s.f. Omission, oversight, inadvertency; failing, failure; miss; mistake, blunder, error, fault.

def: s.f. Potter's clay; clay.

ety: ćun˚ = Prk. चुण(इ) = S. चिनो (ति), rt. चि
def: v.t. To gather, pick, pluck; to choose, select, cull, glean; to pick up (food, &c. as a bird); to place in order, to arrange, to lay (dishes on a table); to gather, to plait (cloth), to crimp; to build up (a door-way, &c.) = ćinnā, q.v.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To pick out, select, choose, &c.; to build up (a doorway, &c.)
v.t. (intens.) To pick out, &c.; to pluck out, pull out
wety: Inherited from Prakrit चिणेइ (ciṇei), from Sanskrit चिनोति (cinoti). Doublet of چُنْنَا (cunnā) and related to بِینْنا (bīnnā, “to pluck”).

def: s.m. The thread-worm, Ascaris; — ćunne, pl. Ascarides.
s.f. A small ruby; — the worm Ascaris (pl. ćunniyāṅ, Ascarides).

s.f. = ćunrī, q.v. s.f. A mode of dying cloth parti-coloured (knots being tied in different parts of it previous to dipping it in the dye to prevent those parts from receiving the colour; syn. ćuṭkī); cloth dyed parti-coloured; a parti-coloured sheet or sāṛī (worn by women).

s.f. A mode of dying cloth parti-coloured (knots being tied in different parts of it previous to dipping it in the dye to prevent those parts from receiving the colour; syn. ćuṭkī); cloth dyed parti-coloured; a parti-coloured sheet or sāṛī (worn by women).

ety: S. चुण्ट्+अ+कः; but cf. ćyūṅṭā, and ćimṭā
def: s.m. A large ant.

ety: ćūnṭ˚ = Prk. चुंट(इ) or चुंटे(इ) = S. चुण्ट(ति) or चुण्टय(ति), rt. चुण्ट्
def: v.t. To press, nip, pinch; to break, pluck, gather (a flower, &c.); — to claw, scratch, tear, rend.
s.f. (prov.) = ćūṅṭī, ćyūṅṭī, q.v.
s.f. A small ant ( = ćyūṅṭī).

ety: ćūnṭ˚ = Prk. चुंट(इ) or चुंटे(इ) = S. चुण्ट(ति) or चुण्टय(ति), rt. चुण्ट्
def: v.t. To press, nip, pinch; to break, pluck, gather (a flower, &c.); — to claw, scratch, tear, rend.
s.m. (local), Head man of a district (cf. ćaudhrī).
s.f. Price paid for picking, &c.; cost of building (a wall, &c.), wages of masons.
adj. Gathered, picked, culled, selected, chosen.
v.t. To cause to be gathered or picked, &c. (see ćunnā).

ety: cf. S. चुप् 'to move quietly or stealthily'
def: s.f. Silence, quiet, stillness; — adj. & adv. Quiet, silent; secret, stealthy; — quietly, silently, &c.; — intj. Silence! be quiet! ( = ćup rah)
subs: ćup-ćup, ćup-ćāp, adj. & adv. Quite silent, perfectly quiet or still: silently, softly, quietly, secretly, furtively, stealthily
ćup sādhnā, ćup lagānā, v.n. To remain silent, to practice silence, to be dumb
v.n. To be or become silent, be speechless; to be silenced.

ety: for shū = Zend fshu = S. पशु+bān, q.v.
def: s.m. A shepherd; a cowherd.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چوپَان (čōpān).

def: s.f. The office of shepherd or cowherd.

ety: Prk. चोप्पडो; prob. fr. S. च्यु(च्यवते)
def: s.m. Oil, grease, butter (see ćupaṛnā).

ety: ćupar˚ = Prk. चोप्पड(इ); prob. fr. S. च्यावय(ति), caus. fr. rt. च्यु
def: v.t. To oil, butter, grease, lubricate, besmear, anoint; to baste; to varnish; to smooth; to gloss over, to palliate.
v.t. = ćupaṛnd, q.v.
v.t. = ćupaṛnd, q.v.
adj. Oiled, oily, greasy, &c. ( = ćupṛā)
v.t. (local) = ćupaṛnā, q.v.
v.n. To move quietly or stealthily; to keep perfectly quiet or silent.
adv. Perfectly silent, quite still, silently, &c. = ćup-ćāp, q.v.s.v. ćup.
adj. (f. -ī), Silent, quiet, mute; secret, stealthy, sly; — ćupke, adv. Silently, quietly, with little noise; secretly, stealthily, clandestinely, furtively, slyly
subs: adv. Quietly, &c. ( = ćupke); in a low voice, in a whisper

def: s.f. Silence ( = ćup)
subs: (-ko), To have a fit of silence; to be struck dumb.

def: s.f. Silence, taciturnity (cf. ćup, and ćupkī).
adj. (f. -ī), Oiled, greasy; oily, unctuous, smooth, plausible, specious, oily-tongued, mealy-mouthed; — ćupṛī, s.f. = ćupṛī roṭī, q.v.
subs: s.f. Smooth or honeyed words, flattery
s.f. A cake rubbed over with, or dipped in, clarified butter.

def: adj. & s.f. See s.v. ćupṛā. adj. (f. -ī), Oiled, greasy; oily, unctuous, smooth, plausible, specious, oily-tongued, mealy-mouthed; — ćupṛī, s.f. = ćupṛī roṭī, q.v.
subs: s.f. Smooth or honeyed words, flattery
s.f. A cake rubbed over with, or dipped in, clarified butter.

def: adj. = ćupṛā, ćupṛī, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Oiled, greasy; oily, unctuous, smooth, plausible, specious, oily-tongued, mealy-mouthed; — ćupṛī, s.f. = ćupṛī roṭī, q.v.
subs: s.f. Smooth or honeyed words, flattery
s.f. A cake rubbed over with, or dipped in, clarified butter.

def: s.f. An imitative sound; cf. ćar, and ćaṛ.
s.f. Vulva ( = ćūt; bur)
subs: s.f. A strumpet; a term of abuse.

def: s.m. Powder; filings; atoms; — part. adj. ( = S. ćūrṇitā), Bruised, crushed, pulverized, powdered; — steeped (in), besotted; exhausted; — powder; filings; atoms
subs: ćur-ćūr, adj. Broken to atoms, utterly crushed, shivered; — ćur-ćār, s.m. Fragments; crumbs
v.t. To break into small pieces, to shatter; to reduce to powder
v.n. To be broken into small pieces, to be shivered; to be reduced to powder; to be exhausted
To be enamoured (of), to dote; to be steeped (in liquor), be intoxicated, be besotted
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi چور (cvr /⁠cor⁠/) (c. 1392), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭 (cora), from Sanskrit चोर (corá), चौर (caurá). Compare Romani chor.

def: s.m. The ceremony of tonsure; a top-knot; a crest; a diadem; a ring, an amulet; the bracelet put on a bride at her marriage; the gold or silver ornament worn by Hindū widows; a ring put on the end of an elephant's tusk (cf. ćūṛā, ćūṛī).
s.m. Powder; filings; sawdust; bruised grain (to be chucked into the mouth); a small piece, a fragment
subs: v.t. To break to atoms; to reduce to powder; to bruise, to pulverize

def: s.m. = ćūrā, q.v. s.m. Powder; filings; sawdust; bruised grain (to be chucked into the mouth); a small piece, a fragment
subs: v.t. To break to atoms; to reduce to powder; to bruise, to pulverize

def: s.f. The ceremony of tonsure; a single lock or tuft of hair left on the crown of the head after the ceremony of tonsure; a top-knot; crest (of a bird, &c.); plume; diadem; head, top, summit
subs: s.m. Shaving the head all but one lock, tonsure
s.m. An ornament or jewel worn on the top of the head; a jewel worn in a crest or diadem.

def: s.m. A top-knot ( = jūṛā); a crest, plume, diadem ( = ćoṭī); an ornament worn on the top of the head; a ring (of brass, &c.) put on the extremity of an elephant's tusk; a ring or bangle of lac (worn by single women and femmes couvertes).

ety: S. चूर्णकः or चिपिटः; cf. ćyūṛā
def: s.m. Rice boiled in the husk, and then parched and pounded
subs: ćūṛā-bhanḍār, s.m. An allowance for the maintenance of the junior members of a zamīndār's family.

def: s.f. The ghost of a woman who dies in a state of pregnancy or of impurity; a witch, hag, beldam; a fury, a shrew; a dirty or ugly old woman, a slut, slattern.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀘𑀼𑀟 (*cuḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀮𑁆𑀮- (-illa-). Cognate with Gujarati ચૂડેલ (cūḍel). Related to چُوت (cūt, “cunt”)، چُڑ (cuṛ, “vulva”)، چُڈّو (cuḍḍo, “harlot”).

def: adj. & s.m. Having a single lock of hair on the crown of the head; having a top-knot; crested; tufted; — a crested one, &c.; — ćūṛālā, s.m. A kind of cyperus, Kyllinga monocephala.
adj. & s.m. Having a single lock of hair on the crown of the head; having a top-knot; crested; tufted; — a crested one, &c.; — ćūṛālā, s.m. A kind of cyperus, Kyllinga monocephala.
adj. & s.m. = ćūrāman, and ćūṛā-maṇī, qq.v.
s.m. Stealing, thieving, robbing.
s.m. = ćūran, q.v. s.m. Stealing, thieving, robbing.

ety: contrac. fr. ćorāwnā; ćorāw˚ = Prk. चोराव(इ), or चोरावे(इ) = S. चोर or चौर+आपय(ति)
def: v.t. To steal, filch, pilfer, rob; to plagiarize; to misappropriate; to suck in or up, to absorb; to draw back; to turn aside or away (the eyes)
subs: To take a furtive glance (at), not to look straight at (one).

def: s.f.(m.?), A wrinkle (in the skin); a crease (in cloth); — (dialec.) envy, jealousy; enmity, spite, grudge, rancour.
adj. = ćūṛāl, q.v. adj. & s.m. Having a single lock of hair on the crown of the head; having a top-knot; crested; tufted; — a crested one, &c.; — ćūṛālā, s.m. A kind of cyperus, Kyllinga monocephala.

ety: S. चूर्ण or H. चूरा redupl.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Crisp, short (like pie-crust), crumbling.
s.f. Dregs, lees, sediment ( = ćurrī).
s.f. A small well.
s.f. A bangle or ring made of lac or glass, &c. ( = ćūṛī, q.v.)
subs: s.m. A maker, or a vendor, of glass or lac bangles.

def: s.f. = ćurī, ćūṛī, q.v.
subs: s.m. See s.v. ćurī. s.f. A small well.

def: s.f. A cake of bread rich with ghī.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭𑀻 (corī), from Sanskrit चौरिका (caurikā).

def: s.f. = ćūnī; and ćūṛī, qq.v. s.f. Bruised or broken grain, pulse coarsely split or ground; — a spark, a small ruby or other gem ( = ćunnī).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭𑀻 (corī), from Sanskrit चौरिका (caurikā).

def: s.f. (dim. of ćūṛā). A ring or bangle of lac or glass (worn on the wrist); a ring; ring (of an eye-glass); a gather, pucker
subs: adj. & s.m. Gathered, puckered; — drawers or sleeves made too long, so as to be crumpled into plaits
s.m. A maker of glass or lac bangles
v.n. 'To put on bangles'; to marry again; — to be effeminate
To break (one's) bangles (as a woman does on the death of her husband; — the verb ṭhanḍī karnā being used superstitiously for toṛnā).
wety: Feminine of چوڑا (cūṛā), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀽𑀟 (cūḍa), from Sanskrit चूड (cūḍa).

def: s.m. Striped cloth.
s.f. A lock of hair; a ringlet, curl.
s.m. Powder.
s.m. A kind of sweatmeat made of crumbled bread or bread-crumbs (ćūr) mixed with butter and sugar.
adj. Crushed, reduced to powder, pulverized (also written ćūrmūr; cf. mūrā-meṭ).
adj. = ćur-mur, q.v. adj. Crushed, reduced to powder, pulverized (also written ćūrmūr; cf. mūrā-meṭ).
s.f. = H چرمرا चुरमुरा ćur-murā, s.m. (onomat.) An imitative sound; murmuring, muttering, fretting, repining, &c.
s.m. Cleaned rice, soaked, flattened, and parched (used as ćubenā).
s.m. = H چرمر चुरमुर ćur-mur, s.f. (onomat.) An imitative sound; murmuring, muttering, fretting, repining, &c.
v.n. To murmur, mutter, fret, moan (over); to be grieved, be vexed (internally).
v.t. To crush, reduce to small pieces or atoms, to reduce to powder.
v.n. To be stolen ( = ćorī honā).

s.m. Powder, any minute particle of substance; flour; dust; aromatic powder, pulvil, pounded sandal, &c.; a digestive powder (composed of ground spices, dried green mangoes, limes, &c.); fossil alkali, efflorescent salt
subs: s.m. A pebble, gravel, hardened fragment of earth or brick; limestone nodule (com. called kaṅkar)

def: v.n. To be troubled with ascarides; to be irritable or peevish; to take ill or amiss.
s.f. Fried or pounded rice, or grain of any kind (eaten dry by chucking into the mouth).
s.f. Dregs, sediment ( = ćurćurī, q.v.). s.f. Dregs, lees, sediment ( = ćurrī).
adj. Wrinkled; creased; — (dialec.) envious, jealous; ill-disposed, mimical, spiteful, malicious, rancorous.
s.m. Rice partially boiled in the husk, and then flattened and parched.
s.f. = ćuruk, q.v. s.f. Chirping (of birds), chirp, chirrup.
v.n. = ćuraknā, q.v.
s.f. Chirping (of birds), chirp, chirrup.
v.n. To chirp, chirrup; to prattle, babble, gabble.
v.t. To cause to steal; to cause to be stolen, get (a thing) stolen.
s.m. One who is in the habit of sucking; (fig.), a tippler.
v.t. To cause to suck, to give to suck.
s.f. A draught or a mouthful of water or other liquid; a suck, a sip; a pull (at a huqqa or pipe)
subs: To take a suck or sip, to sip.

ety: Prk. चूसणअं; S. चूषणीयं rt. चूष्
def: v.t. To suck; to absorb, drink in, draw in; to appropriate; to kiss; to exhaust
subs: v.t. (intens.) To suck dry; to absorb altogether, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit चूषति (cūṣati).

def: v.n. To be sucked, to be absorbed.
s.f. A sucking stick (for children), a (child's) coral; a sucking-bottle ( = ćūsnī).
s.f. A child's coral &c. ( = ćusnī, q.v.). s.f. A sucking stick (for children), a (child's) coral; a sucking-bottle ( = ćūsnī).
s.f. The juice of fruits; anything to be sucked or sipped.
adj. Quick, brisk, active, fleet; smart, adroit, expert, clever, ingenious; narrow, strait, tight, close; compact, well set; fit, suiting; elegant, beautiful; strong, forcible, terse
subs: ćust-o-ćālāk, adj. Active, alert, fleet, agile.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چُسْت (čust).

def: adj. & s.m. = ćusta, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Tight, fitting well (as clothes); — a wrinkle, a crease; — the ventricle of a sheep, or goat, or calf; rennet; tripe; the rectum.
s.f. Agility, alertness, activity, fleetness, nimbleness; speed, expedition, despatch; smartness, dexterity, skill, adroitness, address.
s.m. The mango tree, Mangifera indica; — the anus.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (*cutta).

def: s.f. Vulva
subs: s.f. Fees paid by a bridegroom to the Qāẓī on the morning after his nuptials
s.f. A strumpet; a term of abuse.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (*cutta).

def: s.m. A crack, a snap, a noise.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑁄𑀝𑁆𑀝 (*coṭṭa), ultimately from Sanskrit चुट् (cuṭ, “to cut”).

ety: Prk. चुडक्क(इ) or चुडक्के(इ), fr. S. चुट+कृ
def: v.t. To nip, to bite; to pluck (a flower)
v.t. To wound, to hurt; to attack ( = ćoṭ karnā).
v.t. = ćuṭālnā, q.v. v.t. To wound, to hurt; to attack ( = ćoṭ karnā).
s.m. = ćūtaṛ, q.v.

ety: S. चूत, or च्यूत+ar = Prk. अडो = S. रः
def: The backside, bum, buttock, posteriors, rump, hip
subs: To slap the rump with joy, to be overjoyed
'To beat the buttocks' ( = ćūtāṛ bajānā); — to lament, grieve, bewail
To speak in parables, to use far-fetched expressions; to do wonders
'The circulation of the blood in the posteriors to be stilled'; to be steadied; to be subdued
'To squat down on the hams'; to sit down in a state of helplessness, to be baffled.

ety: S. च्यूत+arī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. dim. of ćūtaṛ, q.v.
subs: To move on the hams; to crawl, creep.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Wounded, stricken, hurt, bruised.
adj. Wounded, stricken, hurt.
adj. (f. -ī), Wounded, stricken, hurt, bruised.
s.f. A lock of hair left on the top of the head when the rest is shaved; a coil of hair, a top-knot
subs: v.n. To die in boyhood (used by women).

def: s.m. One who lives on the earnings of his wife's prostitution; a term of abuse, a blockhead, dolt
subs: ćūtiyā-ćakkar, ćūtiyā-ćampaʼī, s.m. A blockhead, a fool
s.m. A cully, a dupe; — (slang) a man who kills himself by excess of venery, a martyr to venery.

def: s.m. The head of a gang of thieves; a spy to thieves.

ety: ćuṭiyā˚ = S. चुण्ट+इका+ā = āw, caus. augment
def: v.t. To wound, cut, hurt, bruise; to bite; to attack; to beat (metal), to sharpen.
s.m. Foolishness, folly, stupidity.
s.m. = next, q.v.

s.f. A pinch; a handful; snapping the fingers; a knot in cloth (made by a dyer to prevent a part taking the dye); hammer (of a gun); a twist given to lace; stretching cloth after it is sewn; a portion of grain (about two seers) given to a weighman as his fee; a medicinal powder; a ring worn (by women) on any of the toes (except the great toe)
subs: adv. In the time it takes to snap the fingers, in a moment, in a trice
v.n. To produce a snapping sound by means of the thumb and middle finger, to fillip with the fingers (as an accompaniment to a song, &c.)
As much as can be held between a finger and the thumb, a pinch (as of snuff, &c.)
or bhar-lenā, ćuṭkī toṛnā (-kī or -meṅ) = ćuṭkī lenā, q.v.; — ćuṭkī ćaṛhānā (-par), To test the soundness of coin by putting a pinch of testing powder on it ( = ćuṭkī lagānā)
v.t. To nip, to pinch, &c. ( = ćuṭkī lenā)
(-meṅ), To apply the fingers (to), to pick (a pocket); to test coin ( = ćuṭkī ćaṛhānā, q.v.); to fold up (a leaf, &c.); to tear (cloth, &c.) with the tips of the fingers

(-kī or -meṅ), To pinch, nip; to give a fillip (to), to stir up, to kindle; to throw out provoking insinuations or innuendoes; to utter cutting words or sarcasm; to wound or hurt (the feelings, e.g. dil-meṅ ćuṭkī lenā)
v.t. To turn off in a joke; to turn into ridicule.

def: s.m. Jest, pleasantry, banter; facetiousness, humour, wit, a bon mot; a pun; a conundrum; a squib, a mischievous joke or trick; — a specific remedy
subs: v.n. To utter a jest or pleasantry; to let off a squib; to put forth a playfully mischievous story

def: s.m. A cue or lock of hair worn behind; the ribbon with which the hair is tied or braided at the end; the hair so tied or braided ( = ćoṅṭlā); thick large top-knot (of a woman); a coil of false (human) hair (worn by women).
s.f. (colloq.), Sundry or miscellaneous expenses; petty expenditure or contingencies.
v.n. (local), To be frying ( = ćaṭpaṭānā).

s.m. = ćoṭī, and ćuṭiyā, qq.v. s.f. A lock of hair left on the top of the head (the rest being shaven off); the hair braided or plaited and hung down behind, a cue; a coil (of hair), a top-knot; an ornament worn on the head (by women); crest (of a bird); top, summit, acme; peak (of a mountain); pinnacle; apex, vertex; the part by which a balance is suspended
subs: 'To rub the top of the head against the sky'; to be very vain; to be very aspiring or ambitious
adj. Having a ćoṭī; having a crest, crested; having a peak; pointed, tapering
To retain a lock of hair on the top of the head (as a votive offering, &c.); — to overcharge
adj. (f. -ī), The highest, the best, the most excellent, the acme of, the pith or essence of (e.g. ćoṭī-kī bāt)
v.n. To excel, be pre-eminent or unrivalled, &c.
s.m. lit. 'One whose ćoṭī is cut off'; one under control or subjection, a slave
To have the ćoṭī cut off; to be under subjection, to be obedient, to be a slave
ćoṭī gūṅdhnā, To braid or plait the hair
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Having a ćoṭī, &c. ( = ćoṭī-dār); — one who has a ćoṭī; — a ghost, an evil spirit, goblin
(kisī-kī) hāth-meṅ ānā, v.n. To get hold of (one's) cue or top-knot to get (one) into one's power, to have power over one, to subdue.

def: s.f. = ćoṭī or ćuṭiyā, and ćyūṅṭī, qq.v. s.f. s.m. lit. 'One whose ćoṭī is cut off'; one under control or subjection, a slave
To have the ćoṭī cut off; to be under subjection, to be obedient, to be a slave

def: v.t. To cause to be plucked or gathered, to have (flowers, or fruits) plucked.
s.m. The jaw.
s.f. Dropping, dripping; draining; dribbling, dribble; — a windfall (fruit); — a small pit or tank into which water drains, a reservoir, a cistern
subs: s.f. A deep ditch with water springing at the bottom.

def: v.t. To cause to drop or drip, to distil, filter, draw off, drain; — to direct (speech) at, to talk at.
v.n. = ćūnā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Picked, gathered, culled; selected, arranged, laid (as dishes); plaited
s.f. = ćūhī, q.v. s.f. A mouse; a rat.
v.n. = ćūnā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Picked, gathered, culled; selected, arranged, laid (as dishes); plaited
s.m. A young hawk (under a year old), a hawk that has not hunted (cf. ćūza).
s.m. A chicken; a young bird; a young hawk or kite ( = ćūz)
subs: s.f. An old woman who keeps a young gallant, an old woman fond of striplings.

def: s.m. A chicken; a young bird; a young hawk or kite ( = ćūz)
subs: s.f. An old woman who keeps a young gallant, an old woman fond of striplings.

def: adj. (Old H.) = ćār, 'four.'
v.n. (dialec.) = ćūnā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Picked, gathered, culled; selected, arranged, laid (as dishes); plaited
adj. & s.m. Animating, inspiring, promoting; moving, going, — oviparous; — void of virtue or purity, abandoned, wicked; — shaking, concussion; enterprize, exertion; contrivance, management; strength.
s.m. Moving, motion; de parture from, deprivation, loss; sinking, falling, perishing.
s.f. A small ant, an ant.
wety: Feminine of چْیُونْٹا (cyūnṭā).

def: part. (rt. ćyu), Deviated from, erred, strayed; fallen from, fallen off; degraded; dropped; oozed out.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit चैत्र (caitra).

def: s.f. Departing; flowing out; fall; perishing; failing in or falling short of duty; — the vulva; the anus.
s.f. A sickle, bill-hook; iron cleaver.
m. = S دا दा dā, f. (rt. dā), Giving; giver (used as last member of comp., e.g. sukha-da, 'ease-giving'; — janma-da, 'birth-giver,' a father; fem. janma-dā).
f. = S د द da, m. (rt. dā), Giving; giver (used as last member of comp., e.g. sukha-da, 'ease-giving'; — janma-da, 'birth-giver,' a father; fem. janma-dā).

r: ḍa, ṭa-varg, d, drum, r, ṛ, ḍa-kār
ety: when not at the beginning of a syllable
def: s.m. The letter or sound ḍ.
s.m. Custom, manner, habit, wont; institution; affair, business, concern; case, state, condition; — the act of driving vehemently; vehement or forcible propulsion
s.m. = dāv, q.v.
subs: dābānal, see s.v. dāv. s.m. Time, turn; vicissitude; opportunity, chance; (fig.) power, clutch, grasp ( = qābū); — ambuscade, ambush ( = ghāt); snare, trap, stratagem, artifice, trick, manœuvre; sleight or trick (in wrestling); — a stake, wager; throw or cast (of dice); stroke (at a game)
v.n. To lie in ambush, to lurk
v.t. To bring under (one's) power; to get a grip (of one's opponent in wrestling)
dāṅʼo-par lagānā or lagā-denā, v.t. To stake, wager
v.n. To be thrown (dice); to throw high, have a good throw
(apnā), To watch (one's) opportunity; to lie in wait (for)
To make the most of an opportunity, to take advantage
v.n. To have the advantage; — dāṅʼo denā (-ko), To give (one) his turn; to trick, deceive
To practise a sleight or trick (in wrestling)
(-se), To be tricked, be taken in
v.n. To get an opportunity or chance
v.n. To take (one's) turn; — to seize an opportunity

ety: Prk. डब्भो or दब्भो; S. दर्भः
def: s.m. A green cocoa-nut.
s.m. A leather strap or belt, a sword-belt (cf. ḍab, ḍabbā; dabba, and davāl).

ety: Ap. Prk. डवुँ, Prk. दमो; S. दमः; cf. dābnā, dabnā
def: s.m. Strength, power, authority, grasp.

ety: S. दर्वः or दृतिः; cf. dabba; dab-gar; dawāl
def: s.m. Leather (of which oil-pots, scales, spoons, &c. are made); a pouch or pocket; — the nape of the neck
subs: v.t. To pocket, to stow away; — ḍab-gar, s.m. One who makes leathern scales, &c., ( = dab-gar), a worker in leather.

def: s.m. Ostentation, magnificence, pomp. pride.
s.m. Pressure; weight; — power, authority; control, restraint; awe ( = dabāʼo); — impression (in printing, &c.)
subs: dāb jamānā (-par), To bring pressure to bear (on); to exercise control or authority (over), to bring under control, to rule, to control, restrain, subdue.

def: s.m. Covering one (in fever, &c.) with blankets or the like (to produce perspiration); — burden, oppressive burden, oppression, exaction
subs: s.f. Exactions, deductions, or cuttings (from what is lawfully due).

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Pressed down; crouched; alarmed; kept under restraint or control; subdued; snubbed; cowed
subs: adv. With noiseless tread, with silent or soft steps, noiselessly; timidly; furtively, stealthily
adj. (f. -ī), adj. Having a soft or noiseless tread (a man or animal)
adj. (f. dabī-dabāʼī), Covered, concealed; restrained, curbed, repressed; pent-up
adv. In a subdued tone; in a reluctant tone.

ety: dābā(nā+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; and ˚āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: Pressing, pressing down; depression, cavity; compression; pressure; crush; force, strength, power, authority, influence; constraint; suppression, restraint, check, curb; submissiveness, tractableness; resignation; respect; awe, dread
subs: (-kā), To pay regard to authority, to be submissive (to); to submit (to); to stand in awe (of).

def: s.f. = dabāʼo, q.v. Pressing, pressing down; depression, cavity; compression; pressure; crush; force, strength, power, authority, influence; constraint; suppression, restraint, check, curb; submissiveness, tractableness; resignation; respect; awe, dread
subs: (-kā), To pay regard to authority, to be submissive (to); to submit (to); to stand in awe (of).

def: s.m. Water just drawn from a well, fresh water ( = ḍabkā).
s.m. Fear, &c. ( = ḍabkā, q.v.). s.m. Fear, apprehension, dread, awe.
adv. By violence, or force, or compulsion; violently, forcibly, &c.

ety: dabak˚ = Prk. दम्मक्क(इ) or दम्मक्के(इ), fr. S. दम्य˚ (see dabnā)+कृ
def: v.n. To be covered or concealed, to lie hidden or concealed, to skulk; to lurk, to lie in ambush; to crouch; to set (as a dog); to twinkle; to be awed, be intimidated, be cowed, be humbled ( = girgirānā); — v.t. To beat or flatten (wire)
subs: dabak-baiṭhnā, dabak-jānā, v.n. To crouch down, to lie crouching; to skulk, to sneak
v.n. To lie hidden or concealed, to lurk; to crouch down, &c. ( = dabak-jānā).

def: v.n. To shine, glitter, twinkle.

ety: ˚al = Prk. अडो = S. र or ल
def: adj. (rustic) = dabā, q.v. s.m. Covering one (in fever, &c.) with blankets or the like (to produce perspiration); — burden, oppressive burden, oppression, exaction
subs: s.f. Exactions, deductions, or cuttings (from what is lawfully due).

def: adj. = ḍabal, q.v. adj. (rustic) = dabā, q.v. s.m. Covering one (in fever, &c.) with blankets or the like (to produce perspiration); — burden, oppressive burden, oppression, exaction
subs: s.f. Exactions, deductions, or cuttings (from what is lawfully due).

def: s.m. The paisā, or the rupee, made current by the English Government.

ety: dab; prob. past. conj. part. of dabnā
def: v.n. To advance.

v.t. To press down, bow down, compress, depress; to suppress, repress, keep under, put down, restrain, curb, check, chide, snub, awe; to subdue, to tame; to clinch; to crush; — to sow (seed); to make a layer; — dabā-baiṭhnā (-ko), To press down, keep down, to compress; to copulate with; to usurp, to seize upon, to keep possession of by force or fraud
subs: v.t. To conceal, cover up; to subdue, overcome; to encroach upon, to usurp, to keep forcible, or fraudulent, possession of
(dabāʼe = dabā or dabā-ke), v.t. To stow away, hide, keep concealed (generally furtively).

ety: dab(nā) + Prk. अंगं and अंगअं; S. अङ्गं and अङ्गकं
def: adj. & s.m. Ill-formed, ill-fashioned, rough, rude, uncouth, awkward, unpolished; untrained, ill-bred, unpolished, barbarous, brutish; foolish; — a lout, a clown, a dolt; — a blusterer, bully.
wety: Compare دَبَانَا (dabānā) & دَبَاؤ (dabāo).

ety: dab(nā) + Prk. अंगं and अंगअं; S. अङ्गं and अङ्गकं
def: adj. & s.m. Ill-formed, ill-fashioned, rough, rude, uncouth, awkward, unpolished; untrained, ill-bred, unpolished, barbarous, brutish; foolish; — a lout, a clown, a dolt; — a blusterer, bully.

ety: dābā(nā+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; and ˚āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: Pressing, pressing down; depression, cavity; compression; pressure; crush; force, strength, power, authority, influence; constraint; suppression, restraint, check, curb; submissiveness, tractableness; resignation; respect; awe, dread
subs: (-kā), To pay regard to authority, to be submissive (to); to submit (to); to stand in awe (of).

ety: prob. fr. S. डम्बरः; Prk. डंबरो; but cf. next
def: s.m. Low or hollow ground where water collects ( = ḍabrā and ḍahrā), a marsh, swamp, a pool, puddle, a small pond, pit, tank
subs: a vessel for washing the hands in
adj. f. 'Saucer-eyed,' large-eyed; — having eyes that quickly fill with tears, moist-eyed.

def: s.m. Provisions for a journey by water, sea-stock; stores, stock.
adj. & s.m. Pressing down, keeping down; oppressing, oppressive; — pressing heavily in front (as the load of a cart; cf. ulāṛ)

ety: for dabāʼo-nī, fem. of dabāʼonā = dabānā
def: s.f. A pressing-machine; a weight, a paper-weight.

v.t. = dabānā, q.v. v.t. To press down, bow down, compress, depress; to suppress, repress, keep under, put down, restrain, curb, check, chide, snub, awe; to subdue, to tame; to clinch; to crush; — to sow (seed); to make a layer; — dabā-baiṭhnā (-ko), To press down, keep down, to compress; to copulate with; to usurp, to seize upon, to keep possession of by force or fraud
subs: v.t. To conceal, cover up; to subdue, overcome; to encroach upon, to usurp, to keep forcible, or fraudulent, possession of
(dabāʼe = dabā or dabā-ke), v.t. To stow away, hide, keep concealed (generally furtively).

def: s.m. A spoon, ladle (usually of leather); a leathern bottle or vessel (for holding oil, &c.; syn. kuppā; cf. ḍab and ḍabbā).

ety: ḍabb = ḍab, q.v.+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A leathern vessel (for holding oil, ghī, &c.; syn. kuppā); a leather box; a soldier's pouch, cartridge-box; — (fig.) the chest
s.m. Crouching, lying in wait, ambush; — a layer (in a garden, &c.)
subs: To crouch, to lie in wait.

def: s.m. A tanner; a currier.

ety: for A. دابة, orig. a fem. part. n. fr. دبّ 'to creep, to walk,'
def: s.f. A reptile; a beast
subs: 'The Beast of the Earth'; an appellation of one of the signs of the time of the resurrection; the second beast mentioned in the Revelations. (It is believed, by Mohammadans, that it will come forth from aṣ-ṣafa, a mountain at Mecca, with the rod of Moses and the seal of Solomon; with the former it will strike the believer, and with the latter it will stamp the face of the unbeliever, impressing upon it 'This is an unbeliever').

def: s.m. A large spoon, a ladle.

ety: for A. دبدبة dabdabat, fem., inf. n. of دبدب 'to raise cries'; rt. دبّ 'to walk leisurely'
def: s.m. Noise, sound, cry, clamour, din; — (in P., by amplification) ado, state, pomp; dignity, majesty; awfulness, awe.

r: ḍabḍabānā, ḍab˚, ḍub˚, ḍūb, ḍūbnā, lit, āṅkheṅ, āṅsū
def: (by redupl. of ḍab˚, prob. a dial. var. of ḍub˚ = ḍūb, see ḍūbnā), v.n. lit. 'To be brimful, to be overflowing (in which sense it is locally used); to be filled or suffused with tears (the eyes), to be overflowing with tears
subs: (or āṅsū) ḍabḍabānā, The eyes to be filled with tears, to be ready to shed tears.

ety: dabā(nā)+S. कार+कः; or+Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: s.m. A large wooden ploughshare fixed behind the iron share.
s.f. A light kind of plough.
adj. & s.m. Subject; subordinate; weak; — one under subjection or control, a subject.
s.m. A maker of leathern bottles, scales, &c. (see ḍab, and ḍabgar).
s.f. Kuśa-grass (see darbh).
s.m. Kuśa grass, &c. ( = ḍāb, q.v.). s.m. Pressure; weight; — power, authority; control, restraint; awe ( = dabāʼo); — impression (in printing, &c.)
subs: dāb jamānā (-par), To bring pressure to bear (on); to exercise control or authority (over), to bring under control, to rule, to control, restrain, subdue.

def: s.m. A forest.
adj. & s.m. Full of holes and hollows, rugged, uneven (ground); — uneven ground.
part. adj. To be governed, or ruled, capable of being governed or ruled.
s.f. (local), A beam for pressing indigo in the vats or in the frame; — a measure of about ten handfuls (of autumn crop, &c.).
s.f. (dialec.) A reaper's remuneration or allowance (usually a tenth of the harvest).
s.f. lit. 'Made at Dabīq'; a kind of rich silk stuff.
s.m. Name of a medicine; — a paddle.
s.m. A writer, a secretary, notary.
s.m. A school.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَبِسْتَان (dabistān).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to a school; — a student, scholar.
s.m. A measure of about ten handfuls (of corn, &c.).
adj. Thick, coarse, strong (cloth, &c.).
s.m. = ḍābak, q.v.
subs: s.m. Idem. s.m. Water just drawn from a well, fresh water ( = ḍabkā).

ety: prob. ḍab, 'a leathern bag or box'+kā, gen. aff.
def: adj. Fat, corpulent, large (cf. ḍappū).
s.m. Fear, apprehension, dread, awe.
s.f. The act of beating or flattening wire (for embroidery, &c.); — the business or craft of a wire-beater.
s.f. The weight (usually a clod of earth) used to press down the small arm of the ḍheṅklī; — lurking place, covert, ambush.

ety: caus. of dabaknā; dabkā˚ = dabak+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To stow away, to conceal, hide; — to check, chide, snub, threaten, browbeat, daunt, awe, cow, intimidate.
s.f. A trap, snare.
adj. & s.m. Crouching lurking, skulking; — a skulker.
s.f. The act of hiding or skulking; crouching; ambush
subs: dabkī mārnā, v.n. To crouch, to lurk, to lie in ambush.

def: adj. (f. -ī) = dabkel, q.v.). adj. & s.m. Crouching lurking, skulking; — a skulker.

ety: dabkā(nā)+aiyā or iyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. इतव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. An artisan whose work consists in flattening silver wire for purposes of embroidery or edging); a wire-beater.
v.t. To press down; to suppress, repress; to rule; to restrain; to snub; to subdue, to tame; to squeeze, hug (see dabāna)
subs: v.t. 'To sit heavily upon'; to repress, keep down; to take unlawful possession of, to usurp; to embezzle; to encroach upon; to do violence to; to violate
v.t. (intens.), To press down; to bury
v.t. To press, to retain by pressure; to keep down; to keep back, withhold; to conceal, secrete; to steal; to keep possession of by force or fraud; to grip (as money)
v.t. To press down; to overpower, to subdue; to seize by force, to usurp, &c. ( = dāb-baiṭhnā); — to outgrow.

ety: dab˚ = Prk. दम्म(इ) or दव्व(इ)? = S. दम्य(ते), pass. fr. rt. दम्
def: v.n. To be pressed down, be compressed; to be depressed, be bowed down; to be squeezed, be crushed, be buried (under); to sink, to fall; to bend (under pressure or weight); — to be concealed, to lie hid, to lurk, to crouch; — to be restrained, be checked, be repressed, be suppressed, be quelled; to be subdued, be tamed, be awed, be cowed, be snubbed; to submit to control or authority, to yield (to force, &c.); to cower; to shrink; — to be kept back, be withheld; to be kept down, be retrenched; — to fall short (in measure, &c.)
subs: v.n. (intens.), To be pressed down; to sink down; to crouch down; to be suppressed, &c. ( = dabnā); to retire, to withdraw
v.n. To be overcome, be subdued; to be awed
v.n. To be crushed; to be suppressed, to be squashed (as a proposal, &c.)
v.n. To be crushed to death
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀩𑁆𑀩𑀇 (dabbaï), from Dravidian.

def: v.t. To press tight, squeeze, nip; to seize, clutch, grasp; — to stow away, conceal, hide
s.m. A flint.
v.t. To drink intoxicating liquor, to tope, to tipple.
s.m. = ḍabrā, q.v.
s.m. Marshy land, a field under water, a pool, puddle, &c. ( = ḍābar, q.v.)
subs: s.m. A hole or pit of water, a puddle, &c.

def: s.m. = ḍabrā, q.v.
s.f. Division of profits among a village community according to their respective shares.
s.f. An earthen water-vessel, a cup, bowl.

ety: fr. dab(nā), and ghus(nā)+ṛū = अडअइओ = S. र+(इ)तृ+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (prov.), Skulking; lying concealed; hidden, concealed; — weak, helpless, insignificant; — under obligation, dependent; — a skulker, &c.
s.m. A large spoon, a ladle.
s.m. West wind, zephyr.
s.m. A club, a mace.
s.m. The cuddy (of a ship); cabin; stern (of a ship).
s.m. The cuddy (of a ship); cabin; stern (of a ship).
adj. Thick, coarse, strong (as cloth; = dabīz).
(prob. fr. S. दक्ष), s.m.(?) A homestead (Eastern Oudh).
adj. = daćh, and dakshī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Learned, scientific, dexterous, clever, able; — name of an ancient sage (see daksh).
s.m. The south
subs: (S. dakshiṇa+ay˚), s.m. The sun's progress south of the equator, the winter solstice.

def: s.f. Presents to Brāhmans on solemn or sacrificial occasions, fee for spiritual services.
adj. & s.m. = dakshinīya, q.v. adj. Worthy of the sacrificial fee; meriting a reward; worthy to be honoured with presents.
adj. & s.m. Learned, scientific, dexterous, clever, able; — name of an ancient sage (see daksh).
s.m. Ringworm, herpes; the shingles; St. Anthony's fire
subs: dād-mār, s.m. Name of a plant used to cure the ringworm, Cassia alata.

ety: Pehl. dād; Zend dāta, rt. dā; S. datta, rt. dā 'to give'
def: s.f. Giving; gift; — (in comp.) given, &c. ( = dāda, q.v.)
subs: s.f. Liberality, beneficence, bounty, munificence; a present
dād-sitād, s.f. 'Giving and taking,' buying and selling, commerce, dealings ( = len-den; ḵẖarīd-o-faroḵẖt).

ety: Pehl. dāt; Zend dāta, rt. dā = S. dhā, 'to put,' &c.
def: s.f. Statute, law; equity; justice; crying out for justice, complaint; revenge
subs: s.m. Distributor of justice, God ( = dādār, q.v.)
s.f. Crying out for help or justice
To obtain justice or redress, to obtain a hearing for (one's) complaint
To demand justice, to seek redress
adj. & s.m. Demanding justice; — a petitioner for justice, a suitor; a complainant, plaintiff, prosecutor
dād-ḵẖẉāhish, s.f. The demanding of justice, application for redress
(-kī), To dispense justice; to do justice (to), to appreciate, to give due praise (to), to praise duly (e.g. dād suḵẖan-kī detā hai); — to make reparation
adj. Administering justice, just; — one who administers justice, a redresser of grievances
s.f. The administering of justice; redress of grievances, redress, deliverance from oppression or wrong-doing
s.f. Cry for help or redress, application for justice
(-kī), To administer justice, do justice (to); to obtain justice or redress; — to respond to cries for help (from), to come to the aid or rescue (of)
adj. & s.m. Just (prince, &c.); — administrator of justice
s.f. Administration of justice; diffusion of justice
To obtain redress, &c. ( = dād pānā; dād-ko pahuṅćnā)

def: s.m. A rapacious animal, beast of prey; — a forest full of game
subs: dad o dām = dām o dad

def: s.m. A paternal grandfather; — an elder brother; a term of veneration or respect addressed to Brāhmans by Jāṭs and Gūjars, &c.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi دادا (dada /⁠dādā⁠/) (c. 1564), from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀤𑀸𑀤𑁆𑀤 (*dādda) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), an onomatopoeic nursery reduplication.

ety: perf. part. of dādan; and = Zend dāta, rt. dā; S. datta (Vedic dāta), rt. dā 'to give'
def: part. Given, bestowed, imparted; — having given (used chiefly in comp., e.g. tāb-dāda, 'heat-imparted,' inflamed).
s.f. A female servant, a nurse (particularly an old nurse that has attended upon one from her youth); a maid-servant.

s.m. = dhadhaććhar, q.v. s.m. lit. 'Burnt letters'; in versification, certain words or letters which are considered by Hindī poets to be unlucky (as ह, ग, न at the beginning of a verse; र, ज, स in the middle; and क, ट, ज्ञ at the end).
v.n. = dhadhaknā, q.v. v.n. To burn fiercely, to blaze, to flare.
s.f. Advances (of money, pay, &c., made to cultivators, labourers, manufacturers, &c.); — debts
subs: s.m. One who has received advances of money.

ety: Old P. dādhār; Pehl. dātār; Zend dātar, rt. dā; S. धातर, rt. धा
def: s.m. The Creator, God.
s.f. (dialec.), Mother-in-law of the mother-in-law of a wife or husband (syn. dadiyā-sās).
s.m. Father-in-law of a wife's or husband's father-in-law.
s.m. = dadhi, q.v. s.m. Sour, thick milk; coagulated milk ( = dahī)
subs: s.f. The plant Clitoria ternatea, or its flower
s.m. The elephant or wood-apple, Feronia elephantum (the fruit having the acid taste of coagulated milk)
s.m. A presentation, or an offering, of milk

or kādauṅ, or kāṅdo (S. dadhi+kardama), s.m. Coagulated milk and clay thrown by persons at each other in sport on the festival of Kr̤ishṇa's birthday; the festival observed on the anniversary of Kr̤ishṇa's birthday ( = janmāshṭamī parb)
s.m. The churning of coagulated milk.

ety: Prk. डड्ढओ or दड्ढओ = S. दग्ध+कः
def: part. Burnt; — s.m. (dialec.) Fire.

s.m. = dhadhaććhar, q.v. s.m. lit. 'Burnt letters'; in versification, certain words or letters which are considered by Hindī poets to be unlucky (as ह, ग, न at the beginning of a verse; र, ज, स in the middle; and क, ट, ज्ञ at the end).
v.n. = dhadhaknā, q.v. v.n. To burn fiercely, to blaze, to flare.
s.m. Sour, thick milk; coagulated milk ( = dahī)
subs: s.f. The plant Clitoria ternatea, or its flower
s.m. The elephant or wood-apple, Feronia elephantum (the fruit having the acid taste of coagulated milk)
s.m. A presentation, or an offering, of milk

or kādauṅ, or kāṅdo (S. dadhi+kardama), s.m. Coagulated milk and clay thrown by persons at each other in sport on the festival of Kr̤ishṇa's birthday; the festival observed on the anniversary of Kr̤ishṇa's birthday ( = janmāshṭamī parb)
s.m. The churning of coagulated milk.

ety: dādh˚ = Prk. दद्ध or दड्ढ = S. दग्ध p.p.p. of rt. दह्
def: v.n. To burn.
s.f. Name of a medicinal plant.
s.f. Paternal grandmother.
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀤𑀸𑀤𑁆𑀤 (*dādda). + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-) First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi دادی (dādī)ِ.

def: s.m. One suing for justice, a plaintiff, complainant
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀤𑀸𑀤𑁆𑀤 (*dādda). + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-) First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi دادی (dādī)ِ.

def: adj. Of, or pertaining to, or related to, a grandfather (dādā)
subs: s.f. Mother-in-law of a husband's or wife's mother-in-law
s.m. Father-in-law a husband's or wife's father-in-law.

def: s.m. Paternal grandfather's house or family; ancestors on the paternal grandfather's side; pedigree, lineage descent.
